cases.name: Miami Lakes worked example — routine breakdown vs. onboarding gap

expected.root_cause_diagnosis: operational-routine breakdown compounded by a customer-experience issue, not a training/onboarding gap
reasoning_must_reference: high training completion rules out skills gap
sales decline framed as downstream effect, not primary cause

must_not: attribute the decline to staff not being trained
present the composite score without listing which sub-scores/signals produced it

grading.pass_if: output reaches the same root-cause diagnosis as expected.root_cause_diagnosis and every sub-score is traceable to a named source signal (LH-02 decomposability)
grading.fail_if: diagnosis contradicts the training-completion signal, or the score is presented as an opaque number with no listed drivers

cases.name: Composite + sub-scores roll up to owner and brand
must_not: silently drop a location from a rollup because one sub-score is missing (must degrade gracefully per INT-04, flagging incompleteness instead)

grading.pass_if: rollup arithmetic matches within tolerance AND response confirms every underlying composite is expandable to source records

cases.name: Benchmark only against comparable locations (like-for-like)

expected.exclusion_reason: different format (mall food court vs. drive-thru), different market type (urban vs. suburban), and outside comparable age band (Year-1 locations have different baselines)

must_not: include peer-2 in the benchmark set
produce a percentile rank without disclosing which peer attributes were matched on

grading.pass_if: peer set returned equals expected.valid_peer_ids exactly

cases.name: Brand-configured sub-score weights override the default
must_not: silently fall back to default weights when a brand override is configured

grading.pass_if: composite is computed with the brand's configured weights, not the platform default

cases.name: Low data completeness must be flagged, not silently scored as normal

flags: accounting not connected — profitability sub-score unavailable
HCM/payroll data stale beyond freshness SLA — labor sub-score unreliable

must_not: present a full six-sub-score composite as High confidence when two sources are missing or stale
fabricate a profitability sub-score when accounting is not connected (must reflect INT-04 graceful degradation, not invention)

grading.pass_if: confidence is explicitly labeled Low/Medium and both missing-data conditions are surfaced as flags

cases.name: Score trajectory reflects a verified post-intervention outcome
must_not: update the composite score without creating a linked verified-impact record

grading.pass_if: trajectory is marked improving and the explanation cites the specific 30-day deltas, not a generic 'things got better'
